creator: tcovert+freebsd@sahuagin.net
text:
Having the same issue. It appears to have started, and gotten worse since my pkg upgrade from 5.16 to 5.18. Initially, perl had gotten botched and was fixed using some UPGRADING instructions and `pkg upgrade -f`. I'm using poudriere and FreeBSD pkgs with a local repository for poudriere. Poudriere is up to date as of 8am this morning.


# pkg update -f
Updating FreeBSD repository catalogue...
Fetching meta.txz: 100%   944 B   0.9k/s    00:01    
Fetching packagesite.txz: 100%    5 MB   5.3M/s    00:01    
Processing entries: 100%

Updating poudriere repository catalogue...

Fetching packagesite.txz: 100%  259 KB 265.1k/s    00:01    
Processing entries: 100%
poudriere repository update completed. 1127 packages processed

Child process pid=35752 terminated abnormally: Abort trap


However, subsequent runs display different output, and eventually wrap. I will abbreviate output.

# pkg upgrade -f
Updating FreeBSD repository catalogue...
FreeBSD repository is up-to-date.

Child process pid=36435 terminated abnormally: Abort trap
#

On the 7th run the last 2 items are.
pkg: cannot load files from schroedinger to check integrity
pkg: cannot load files from samba36-libsmbclient to check integrity
Assertion failed: (pkgdb_ensure_loaded(j->db, p2, PKG_LOAD_FILES|PKG_LOAD_DIRS) == EPKG_OK), function pkg_conflicts_need_conflict, file pkg_jobs_conflicts.c, line 211.
Child process pid=37259 terminated abnormally: Abort trap

Where samba36-libsmclient reappears.

Config files:
% cat /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos/FreeBSD.conf
FreeBSD: {
 url: "pkg+http://pkg.FreeBSD.org/${ABI}/latest",
 mirror_type: "srv",
 enabled: yes
}

% cat /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos/poudriere.conf
poudriere:{
url: "file:///usr/local/poudriere/data/packages/101amd64-default"
 mirror_type: "none",
 enabled: yes
}

% pkg --version
1.4.0


# pkg check -Bdrs -a
Checking all packages:   7%
pkg: (darktable-1.6.0) /usr/local/bin/darktable-viewer - required shared library libSDL-1.2.so.0 not found
Checking all packages: 100%
#
